_comment: Machine-parseable registry of the plugins-factory rubric library. Consume this to discover the 9 holistic dimensions, the 5 plugin-distinctive drill-down rubrics (P2-P6), and their paired foundations. P1/P7/P8/P9 are scored with four rubrics co-located from skills-studio (see `references_colocated`) so the plugin is self-contained — zero cross-plugin paths. The optional `foundation` field links a rubric to its theory doc in foundations/; bin/check-foundations-coverage.py gates that every foundations/*.md has exactly one rubric pointing back to it.

critics.lens: Always-on context cost, vanilla bundle > ceremony, PEV loop
Marketplace as platform, namespacing, plugin granularity
Delete components, smallest viable plugin, first principles
Bundled hook/MCP blast radius, trust posture, lethal trifecta
Manifest correctness, make illegal layout/state unrepresentable
Component-fit determinism boundary, MCP tool contracts
Reproducible packaging, versioning, CI validate gate, idempotency

dimension_types.gate: Scoreable mechanically — validate_plugin.py output, path-legality check, counts, kebab-case. Can be an automated CI check.
dimension_types.review: Requires expert judgment — boundary coherence, component-fit appropriateness, always-on-cost justification.
dimension_types.hypothesis: Stated as an observable property but not yet empirically verified across real plugins. Track with telemetry (install/disable, plugin details cost).

rubrics.name: plugins-holistic
rubrics.key_question: Does this plugin address all nine load-bearing dimensions — fitness, component fit, boundary cohesion, dependency legality, manifest correctness, context economy, routing, evolution, and security?
rubrics.note: Meta-rubric synthesizing all nine load-bearing plugin quality dimensions. P2-P6 are plugin-distinctive (dedicated foundation + drill-down). P1/P7/P8/P9 are scored with rubrics co-located from skills-studio (see references_colocated). Use at author/carve time (does this plugin address all 9?) and at score/promote time.

references_colocated._comment: Decision A (self-contained): co-located from skills-studio into references/rubrics/ — bundled, not referenced across the install boundary; zero `../` cross-plugin paths. VENDORED COPIES as of 2026-06-02 — re-sync when the skills-studio originals change; no automatic drift check yet (tracked in ROADMAP).

rubrics.name: component-fit
rubrics.key_question: Is each capability the right primitive — hook (deterministic guarantee), MCP (external-state intent perimeter), agent (isolation/parallel), skill (auto-invoked knowledge), or command (user-named action)?

rubrics.name: boundary-cohesion
rubrics.key_question: Is the plugin scope one coherent job — neither a kitchen-sink monolith nor a fragment of a workflow — with internal granularity inside the domain?

rubrics.name: dependency-and-sharing
rubrics.key_question: Are cross-plugin dependencies resolved legally — co-located, declared in `dependencies`, or symlinked within the marketplace — with zero `../` paths that break at install, and nothing dead bundled?

rubrics.name: manifest-and-packaging
rubrics.key_question: Is the plugin.json (and marketplace.json) valid, with a correct directory layout, path variables, and persistent-state placement — does it install and update without silent failure?

rubrics.name: context-economy
rubrics.key_question: Is the plugin toggleable with lean always-on cost, and is its bundled knowledge progressively disclosed — or does enabling it tax every unrelated session?

rubrics.name: agent-fit
rubrics.key_question: Is each bundled agent the right primitive (needs isolation / parallelism / tool-reduction), minimally tool-scoped without the lethal trifecta, loader-rule-clean, and — if a reviewer — a distinct cited lens under a sound orchestration?

adoption_contract._comment: Plugins adopting this rubric library should declare which dimensions they score and their current values, enabling adoption tracking and regression detection.
